select_when: Complexity exceeds current capacity and the learner or team can master the next increment but not yet the whole system.
Sequencing, pacing, and support quality will determine whether people build usable competence or just accumulate confusion.
Temporary structure should help people become independent later instead of remaining permanently dependent on a framework.
A complex concept or workflow needs to be made tangible through simpler chunks, examples, and staged handoff.
avoid_when: The scaffold has become permanent bureaucracy that blocks transfer of judgment, autonomy, or true mastery.
A familiar framework is being imposed unquestioningly even though its assumptions do not actually fit the problem.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on staged support, chunking, and temporary structure rather than on broad pedagogy commentary.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor education-program sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on complexity reduction, sequencing, and support withdrawal.
open_questions: The source reaches into consulting and AI workflow design, but the operational file stays focused on staged support transfer.

select_when.note: The raw source explicitly says scaffolding is strongest when complexity exceeds current capacity, sequencing quality matters, and temporary structure can later be removed.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns against permanent scaffolds and against misapplied frameworks through law-of-the-instrument behavior.

input_type.note: The source is explicit about overwhelmed learners and complex tasks, while the machine-usable label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw model delivers staged support and gradual handoff; the output label compresses that into a reusable intervention type.

reasoning_types.note: The model diagnoses capacity limits and organizes the learning system around them, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
